title: Fitting magnetic secondary glazing with acrylic sheet
subtitle: Halve a single-glazed window's heat loss without touching the original frame
excerpt: Magnetic secondary glazing holds a sheet of acrylic in the window reveal, trapping an air gap that drops the combined U-value from around 5.0 to 2.5-3.0 W/m²·K. The panel lifts off for summer ventilation. A standard sash takes around three hours and costs in materials.
sourceNotes: Historic England guidance on secondary glazing in listed buildings (historicengland.org.uk). BRE secondary glazing U-value reference. Energy Saving Trust single vs secondary vs double glazing comparison guidance.

recipeTools.notes: For scoring and snapping acrylic sheet to size.
Only needed if the magnetic channel requires screw-fixing rather than adhesive.

glossaryTerms.definition: A second glazed layer added on the room side of an existing window. Creates a trapped air gap that reduces the combined U-value without replacing or altering the original window — important for listed buildings and rental properties where changing the window is not permitted.

content.text: A single-glazed window has a U-value around 5.0 W/m²·K. Adding a sheet of 4 mm acrylic in a magnetic channel on the room side creates a sealed air gap of 20-100 mm. The combined assembly acts as
secondary glazing
and drops the effective U-value to 2.5-3.0 W/m²·K. The panel pulls off magnetically for cleaning and summer ventilation.

attrs.title: Listed buildings
attrs.body: Secondary glazing installed on the room side does not alter the external appearance of the window. It generally does not require listed building consent. Confirm with your local planning authority before starting if you are unsure.

content.text: What you need
Acrylic sheet, 4 mm clear, order cut to size or score-and-snap from a stock sheet.
Magnetic secondary glazing channel kit, soft magnetic strip for the reveal, metal carrier strip for the acrylic edge. Most kits include adhesive.
Contact adhesive or double-sided foam tape if not included in the kit.
Tape measure, pencil, metal straight edge.
Measuring and cutting
Step 1, measure the reveal.

Measure the inside face of the window reveal, width and height at three points each and take the smallest measurement. Subtract 5 mm each way for channel thickness. Note whether the reveal is plaster (bond with adhesive) or timber (adhesive or screw fix).
Step 2, cut the acrylic.
Score deeply along a metal straight edge with a sharp utility knife and snap over a table edge. Wear eye protection, acrylic splinters are sharp. Peel the protective film only after fitting.
Fitting
Step 3, bond the magnetic strip to the reveal.
Wipe the reveal faces clean and dry. Press the magnetic strip to all four faces, working corner to corner. Ensure continuous contact, any gap lets cold air bypass the panel.
Step 4, fix the metal carrier to the acrylic edge.
Apply the metal strip to the perimeter edge of the acrylic sheet using the adhesive supplied. Hold for the full cure time before engaging the magnets.
Step 5, hang and test.

Offer the acrylic panel to the reveal. The magnetic strips pull it flush. Run your hand around the perimeter, there should be no draught with the panel in place. Peel the protective film from both faces.

items.symptom: Condensation forming between acrylic and window glass
items.cause: Air gap not fully sealed, warm room air is entering and condensing on the cold outer glass
items.fix: Check all four edges for gaps. If the reveal face is uneven, pack behind the magnetic strip with thin foam tape to close any step.

items.symptom: Acrylic bowing away from the window in the centre
items.cause: Sheet cut too large, or temperature differential bowing the acrylic
items.fix: Ensure the sheet is cut 5 mm undersize each way. For windows wider than 800 mm, add a mid-span magnetic support button.

items.symptom: Channel adhesive failing from plaster reveal
items.cause: Plaster surface dusty or recently decorated
items.fix: Remove channel, clean with dry cloth, prime with dilute PVA, allow to dry, re-bond with contact adhesive.
